content.text:
<user_query>
You are an expert creative frontend developer specializing in immersive 3D web experiences using Three.js, GSAP, and WebGL. Build a stunning, award-worthy 3D portfolio website for Rudra Pratap Singh (GitHub: DevRudrax) — a Computer Science Engineering student and AI Developer passionate about Machine Learning, LLM fine-tuning, and systems programming.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🎨 VISUAL DIRECTION & AESTHETIC
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Inspired by aerukart.com — a cinematic, dark-themed, full-screen 3D experience. Think:
- Deep space / neural network aesthetic — dark background (#0a0a0f) with glowing neon accents in electric blue (#00d4ff) and violet (#8b5cf6)
- Floating 3D geometric particles forming constellations or neural node networks in the background (Three.js)
- Smooth scroll-driven 3D camera movement — sections feel like you're flying through a digital universe
- Heavy use of GSAP ScrollTrigger for cinematic transitions between sections
- Distortion / morphing text effects on headings (SplitText or custom shaders)
- Magnetic cursor with trail effect
- Glassmorphism cards with subtle neon borders and depth
- Grain/noise overlay texture for cinematic feel

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📐 TECH STACK
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
- Three.js (WebGL 3D canvas as full-page backdrop)
- GSAP + ScrollTrigger (all animations and scroll-based transitions)
- Vanilla JS or React (your choice)
- Custom GLSL shaders for particle effects and hover distortions
- CSS Variables + modern CSS for layout

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🏗️ PAGE STRUCTURE & SECTIONS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

[1] HERO / LANDING
- Full-screen 3D canvas with an animated neural-network particle field
- Large bold name: "RUDRA PRATAP SINGH" with a glitch/reveal animation on load
- Animated subtitle that types out: "AI Developer · LLM Engineer · Systems Builder"
- A glowing CTA button: "Explore My Universe →" that triggers scroll
- Subtle floating 3D orbs / geometric shapes in the background

[2] ABOUT
- Camera "flies into" a new section on scroll
- Short punchy bio: CS Engineering student building AI-powered solutions, LLM fine-tuning pipelines, and SaaS products
- A 3D rotating wireframe sphere or cube beside the text (Three.js)
- Skill tags float in as animated pills: Python, Rust, C, React, Next.js, Node.js, MongoDB, AWS, Firebase, Oracle, NumPy, Pandas

[3] PROJECTS
- 3D card carousel or horizontal scroll with project cards that tilt on hover (VanillaTilt.js or custom)
- Projects to feature (pull from GitHub: github.com/DevRudrax):
  • PS_DualMindCalculator — Python AI calculator
  • BMI Calculator — Python CustomTkinter with dark UI & health visualization
  • DevRudrax-prs-fitness — Fitness tracker
  • (Placeholder card: "More coming soon..." with a pulsing animation)
- Each card: project name, tech stack badges, GitHub link button with hover glow

[4] SKILLS / TECH STACK
- Animated 3D floating tech logos orbiting a central glowing sphere (Three.js orbit animation)
- Or: a particle burst animation where logos appear as nodes in a network graph
- Categories: Languages (Python, Rust, C), Frontend (React, Next.js, HTML5), Backend (Node.js, MongoDB, MySQL), Cloud (AWS, Firebase, Oracle)

[5] CONTACT
- Dark glassmorphism panel
- Text: "Let's build something extraordinary."
- Two glowing icon buttons side-by-side:
  → LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/rudra-pratap-singh-bb6b79324
  → GitHub: github.com/DevRudrax
  → Email: r.p.singh7439@gmail.com
- Optional: minimal contact form with floating label inputs

[6] FOOTER
- "© 2025 Rudra Pratap Singh · Built with passion & Three.js"
- Subtle looping particle animation

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
✨ KEY INTERACTION DETAILS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
- Custom cursor: glowing dot that morphs to a ring on hoverable elements
- Smooth page load sequence: black screen → logo/name reveals with particle burst
- All scroll transitions must feel cinematic — no abrupt jumps
- Mobile responsive (simplified 2D fallback for Three.js on mobile for performance)
- Preloader screen with animated "RPS" monogram or progress bar

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📌 PERSONAL DETAILS TO EMBED
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
- Name: Rudra Pratap Singh
- Alias: DevRudrax
- Role: AI Developer | Python Programmer | LLM Enthusiast | SaaS Builder
- GitHub: https://github.com/DevRudrax
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/rudra-pratap-singh-bb6b79324/
- Email: r.p.singh7439@gmail.com
- Instagram: @orbitrider

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🎯 QUALITY STANDARD
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
The final result should feel like an Awwwards nominee — every detail polished, every animation purposeful, every section earning its place. No generic templates. This should look like the portfolio of someone who builds AI systems and thinks at the frontier of technology.
</user_query>

input.prompt: In directory C:\Users\user\.cursor\projects\empty-window, run: npm install && npm run build. Write full output to C:\Users\user\.cursor\projects\empty-window\build-log.txt. Report success or errors.

content.text:
Your **3D portfolio for Rudra Pratap Singh (DevRudrax)** is ready at:

`C:\Users\user\.cursor\projects\empty-window`

## What you get

A cinematic, dark-space portfolio inspired by aerukart-style experiences:

| Layer | Implementation |
|--------|----------------|
| **3D backdrop** | Full-screen Three.js neural particle field with custom GLSL shaders, connection lines, and floating wireframe orbs |
| **Scroll** | Lenis smooth scroll + GSAP ScrollTrigger (camera fly-through, section reveals, pinned horizontal project carousel on desktop) |
| **Hero** | Glitch-style character reveal, typewriter subtitle, magnetic CTA |
| **About** | Rotating wireframe icosahedron + animated skill pills |
| **Projects** | Glass cards with tilt hover, GitHub links for your repos + “More coming soon” pulse card |
| **Skills** | 3D orbital tech labels around a glowing core (2D fallback on mobile) |
| **Contact** | Glass panel, LinkedIn / GitHub / Email / Instagram, floating-label form |
| **Polish** | RPS preloader + particle burst, custom cursor + trail, grain + vignette, scroll progress nav |

## Run locally

```bash
cd C:\Users\user\.cursor\projects\empty-window
npm install
npm run dev
```

Vite opens the site (usually `http://localhost:5173`).

## Project structure

```
empty-window/
├── index.html          # All sections + content
├── src/
│   ├── main.js         # Orchestration
│   ├── styles/main.css # Design system (#0a0a0f, cyan/violet)
│   ├── shaders/        # neural.vert / neural.frag
│   └── js/
│       ├── neural-scene.js
│       ├── about-mesh.js
│       ├── skills-orbit.js
│       ├── animations.js
│       ├── smooth-scroll.js
│       ├── preloader.js + preloader-burst.js
│       ├── cursor.js, tilt.js, footer-particles.js
```

## Highlights

- **Preloader**: RPS monogram → progress bar → cyan/violet particle burst → hero reveal  
- **Desktop projects**: Section pins while you scroll horizontally through cards  
- **Mobile**: Reduced WebGL density, touch-friendly horizontal scroll, no custom cursor  
- **Personal links**: GitHub, LinkedIn, email, and @orbitrider embedded in Contact  

If you want next steps—deploy to Vercel/Netlify, add a real form backend (Formspree/EmailJS), or tune performance further—say what you prefer.

[REDACTED]

PowerShell is blocking `npm.ps1` due to execution policy. Installing dependencies with `npm.cmd` (bypasses the script wrapper):

[REDACTED]

input.command: cd "C:\Users\user\.cursor\projects\empty-window" && npm.cmd install && npm.cmd run build
input.description: Install deps via npm.cmd and build

content.text:
PowerShell is blocking `npm` because it tries to run `npm.ps1`, and your **execution policy** disallows scripts.

## Quick fix (use in your terminal)

Run install with the `.cmd` wrapper instead:

```powershell
cd C:\Users\user\.cursor\projects\empty-window
npm.cmd install
npm.cmd run dev
```

That avoids `npm.ps1` entirely.

## Permanent fix (recommended)

Allow local scripts for your user only (no admin needed):

```powershell
Set-ExecutionPolicy -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -Scope CurrentUser
```

Confirm with `Y`, then:

```powershell
npm install
npm run dev
```

## Alternative: use Command Prompt

Open **cmd** (not PowerShell) in the project folder:

```cmd
cd C:\Users\user\.cursor\projects\empty-window
npm install
npm run dev
```

---

After install succeeds, Vite should open the portfolio at `http://localhost:5173`. If anything else fails after `npm install`, paste the new error and we can fix it.

[REDACTED]

Investigating why the page shows only a dark screen — likely a stuck preloader, a JS error, or opening the file without Vite.

[REDACTED]

Identifying issues: opening `index.html` directly prevents JS from loading (blank screen), and a preloader burst bug plus GSAP errors can leave the page stuck or invisible. Fixing these now:

[REDACTED]

<script type="module" src="/src/main.js"></script>
  </body>
</html>

'Then open the localhost URL — do not double-click index.html.

';

';

content.text:
The site **is working**, but two things were making it look broken:

## 1. Most common cause: opening `index.html` directly

This project is a **Vite app**. It cannot run by double-clicking `index.html`.

- ES module imports (`import gsap from 'gsap'`) do not load from `file://`
- JavaScript never runs → preloader stays black, hero text stays invisible (`opacity: 0`)

**You must use the dev server:**

```powershell
cd C:\Users\user\.cursor\projects\empty-window
npm.cmd install
npm.cmd run dev
```

Then open the URL Vite prints (usually **http://localhost:5173**), not the file path.

---

## 2. Bugs that could freeze or blank the screen (now fixed)

| Issue | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| **Preloader burst** drew particles with a **negative radius** → console error | Could interrupt startup |
| **GSAP** targeted empty NodeLists | Warnings; some animations skipped |
| **No fallback** if preloader/animations failed | Black screen with hidden content |

Fixes applied: safer burst animation, GSAP guards, 4.5s preloader timeout, and CSS so content shows once loaded. If you open via `file://`, you’ll now see instructions instead of a blank screen.

---

## What you should see when it’s correct

1. Brief **RPS** preloader (~2 seconds)  
2. Particle burst, then hero: **RUDRA PRATAP SINGH** + neural 3D background  
3. Scroll reveals About, Projects, Skills, Contact  

---

## Quick checklist

1. Terminal in project folder  
2. `npm.cmd run dev` (dev server running)  
3. Browser at **http://localhost:5173**  
4. Hard refresh: **Ctrl + Shift + R**  
5. If still stuck: **F12 → Console** and share any red errors  

If you’re already on `localhost:5173` and still see only black, tell me whether you see **“INITIALIZING UNIVERSE”** or a completely empty screen—that narrows it down.

[REDACTED]

Investigating the glitches in the browser and code to identify and fix the causes.

[REDACTED]

The glitches come mainly from Lenis and ScrollTrigger fighting each other, aggressive pinned horizontal scroll, jittery camera motion, and overlapping effects. Fixing these systematically:

[REDACTED]

content.text:
The glitches came from **Lenis and ScrollTrigger fighting each other**, plus a few heavy effects stacked on top. Here’s what was fixed:

## Root causes fixed

| Problem | Fix |
|--------|-----|
| **Lenis + ScrollTrigger mismatch** | Added `ScrollTrigger.scrollerProxy()` so scroll position stays in sync |
| **Pinned horizontal projects section** | Removed GSAP pin (was jumping/overlapping sections) → normal vertical scroll + horizontal card strip |
| **Animations reversing on scroll up** | Switched to `once: true` so content doesn’t flicker in/out |
| **Glitch headings re-firing** | Glitch runs **once per heading**, softer effect, ghosts hidden when idle |
| **Jittery 3D camera** | Smoothed scroll-driven camera (lerp, no rapid sine wobble) |
| **Cursor `mix-blend-mode`** | Removed difference blend (was inverting colors oddly) |
| **Magnetic nav + elastic bounce** | Disabled magnetic on nav; gentler motion on buttons |
| **Hero 3D rotateX** | Simpler reveal (less text distortion) |

## What you should do

1. **Hard refresh** the page: `Ctrl + Shift + R`
2. Make sure dev server is running:
   ```powershell
   cd C:\Users\user\.cursor\projects\empty-window
   npm.cmd run dev
   ```
3. Open **http://localhost:5173** (not the raw `index.html` file)

You should get smooth scrolling, stable sections, readable About text, and no layout jumping in Projects.

If anything still looks off, tell me **when** it happens (scroll, hover, preloader, etc.) and I’ll target that next.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
smooth scroll not happening very much buggy
</user_query>

Diagnosing the Lenis + ScrollTrigger integration and fixing smooth scroll.

[REDACTED]

input.explanation: Find current best practice for Lenis + ScrollTrigger integration

content.text:
Fixing Lenis integration: `lenis.raf()` expects milliseconds but was receiving GSAP's seconds, and `scrollerProxy` conflicted with Lenis's transform-based scroll. Rewriting smooth scroll using the official pattern.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Smooth scroll was broken because **Lenis was wired incorrectly** to GSAP. That’s fixed now.

## What was wrong

1. **Wrong time units** — `lenis.raf()` needs milliseconds (`performance.now()`). It was getting GSAP’s time in seconds, so scroll barely moved and felt stuck.
2. **`scrollerProxy` conflict** — It fought Lenis’s own transform-based scroll and caused jitter.
3. **Extra ScrollTrigger scrub** on `documentElement` — Duplicated scroll handling and made the 3D camera stutter.
4. **Mismatched Lenis CSS** — CDN vs npm package could disagree.

## What changed

- Official Lenis + GSAP pattern: `lenis.on('scroll', ScrollTrigger.update)` + `performance.now()` in the ticker
- **`lerp: 0.085`** for smooth, natural wheel scroll (instead of conflicting duration/easing)
- Scroll progress from **`lenis.progress`** (single source of truth)
- **`data-lenis-prevent`** on the projects carousel so horizontal scroll doesn’t fight vertical smooth scroll
- Native scroll on touch devices (Lenis off on mobile — it’s often buggy there)

## Try it

Hard refresh: **Ctrl + Shift + R**

```powershell
npm.cmd run dev
```

Open **http://localhost:5173** — wheel scroll should feel smooth and continuous, and nav links should glide to sections.

If it still feels off, say whether you’re on **trackpad or mouse wheel** and I can tune `lerp` / `wheelMultiplier`.

[REDACTED]
